description: Fix docs site UI bugs, accessibility issues, dead code, and CSS problems found during codebase audit.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Fix TypeScript type bugs and date format error
userStories.description: As a docs site visitor, I want correct rendering (proper year display) and as a developer I want correct TypeScript types.
acceptanceCriteria: h5 and h6 in MDX.tsx use `HTMLAttributes<HTMLHeadingElement>` (not `HTMLHeadElement`)
The date format in `docs/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx` uses `yyyy` (4 y's) not `yyy` (3 y's)
The footer copyright year in `docs/components/footer.tsx` uses `new Date().getFullYear()` instead of hardcoded `2025`
The Badge component interface in `docs/components/retroui/Badge.tsx` is named `BadgeProps` (not `ButtonProps`)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Four small fixes in four files:

1. docs/components/MDX.tsx lines 42 and 45: Change `HTMLHeadElement` to `HTMLHeadingElement` for h5 and h6 components. h1-h4 already use the correct type on lines 30-39.

2. docs/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx line 47: Change the date-fns format string from `'dd MMM, yyy'` to `'dd MMM, yyyy'`. Three y's is a 'local week-numbering year' which produces wrong output.

3. docs/components/footer.tsx line 46: Replace `© 2025 OpenTabs` with `© {new Date().getFullYear()} OpenTabs`.

4. docs/components/retroui/Badge.tsx line 26: Rename `interface ButtonProps` to `interface BadgeProps`. Update the component function signature to use `BadgeProps` instead of `ButtonProps`.

userStories.title: Remove dead code: blog components, stale IDs, unused CSS classes, broken image URLs
userStories.description: As a codebase maintainer, I want dead code removed so the docs site is lean and there are no broken references.
acceptanceCriteria: The `blogComponents` object and `type?: 'doc' | 'blog'` parameter are removed from MDX.tsx (no blog content exists)
The `id='cody-copy-button'` and `data-umami-event` attributes are removed from CodeBlock.tsx
The broken `--background-image` CSS variable values referencing `/images/banner_void_2.svg` and `/images/bg_void_3.svg` are removed from global.css (the files don't exist)
The `.bg-image` and `.text-outlined` CSS classes are removed from global.css (never used)
The `--chart-*` CSS variables are removed from global.css (never referenced)
The RetroUI remote image patterns (`pub-5f7cbdfd9ffa4c838e386788f395f0c4.r2.dev` and `cms.retroui.dev`) are removed from next.config.mjs
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Dead code cleanup across several files:

1. docs/components/MDX.tsx:
   - Remove `blogComponents` (lines 113-124). Remove `type` parameter from MDX function (line 123). Simplify to always use `docComponents`.

2. docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx:
   - Line 27: Remove `id='cody-copy-button'` — vestigial from a template.
   - Line 28: Remove `data-umami-event='copy-code-button'` — no Umami analytics installed.

3. docs/app/global.css:
   - Lines 60, 86, 111: Remove `--background-image` declarations with broken `/images/*.svg` URLs. No images directory exists in public/.
   - Lines 338-342: Remove `.bg-image` class (never used).
   - Lines 344-351: Remove `.text-outlined` class (never used).
   - Lines 34-38 and in `:root` and `.dark` blocks: Remove `--chart-1` through `--chart-5` variables (never referenced by any component).

4. docs/next.config.mjs lines 13-22: Remove the `images.remotePatterns` array with RetroUI CDN domains. No image in the codebase uses these domains.

userStories.title: Fix CSS bugs: scrollbar colors, WebKit scrollbar visibility, syntax highlighting
userStories.description: As a docs site visitor, I want scrollbars to be visible and syntax highlighting to be readable across all themes.
acceptanceCriteria: Firefox scrollbar track uses `var(--background)` or `transparent` instead of `var(--foreground)`
WebKit scrollbar thumb in `.sidebar-scroll` becomes visible on container hover (not permanently transparent)
The WebKit scrollbar uses a non-transition approach (e.g., parent hover selector) since pseudo-element transitions don't work
Syntax highlighting in `.hljs` uses fixed colors from the code-bg palette rather than theme-dependent CSS variables, so code blocks remain readable in all color themes
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Three CSS fixes in docs/app/global.css:

1. Line 335: `scrollbar-color: var(--muted) var(--foreground)` — the second value is the track color. `--foreground` is black in light mode, making the scrollbar track a black bar. Change to `scrollbar-color: var(--muted) transparent` or `scrollbar-color: var(--muted) var(--background)`.

2. Lines 442-455: The `.sidebar-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb` has `background: transparent` and a `transition`. CSS transitions don't work on ::-webkit-scrollbar pseudo-elements. Replace with a parent hover approach:
   ```css
   .sidebar-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: transparent; }
   .sidebar-scroll:hover::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--muted); }
   ```
   Remove the non-functional `transition` property.

3. Lines 365-428: The `.hljs` syntax highlighting rules use theme CSS variables like `var(--primary)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--muted-foreground)`. These change across themes, so code could become unreadable with certain color palettes (e.g., red theme makes keywords red on dark background). Replace with fixed Dracula/One Dark-style colors that work on the `--code-bg` background regardless of theme. Keep `--code-bg` as the background variable (it's consistent), but use fixed foreground colors for tokens.

userStories.title: Improve accessibility: skip nav, hamburger menu, mobile overlay
userStories.description: As a keyboard or screen reader user, I want proper navigation and focus management so I can use the docs site effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Skip to main content' link is the first focusable element on the page, hidden until focused
The hamburger menu button has `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and `aria-label` attributes
The mobile menu closes when pressing the Escape key
The mobile menu overlay uses `fixed` positioning (not `absolute`) to cover the full viewport regardless of scroll position
Body scroll is prevented when the mobile menu is open
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Accessibility improvements across 3 files: 1. docs/app/layout.tsx: Add a skip navigation link as the first child of ``. Pattern: ```html Skip to main content ``` Also add `id="main-content"` to the `
` element in the docs layout. 2. docs/components/HamburgerMenu.tsx: - Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` and `aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close menu' : 'Open menu'}` to the button. - Add `useEffect` for Escape key handler: `document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') onClose(); })`. - Change overlay from `absolute` to `fixed` positioning. - Add body scroll lock: `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` when open, restore on close/unmount. - Add `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` to the menu container.

userStories.title: Remove unused theme variant infrastructure
userStories.description: As a codebase maintainer, I want unused color theme code removed since it adds complexity without being used.
acceptanceCriteria: The `colorTheme`, `variant`, `setColorTheme`, `setVariant` state and functions are removed from ThemeContext.tsx (only `isDarkMode` and `toggleDarkMode` are kept)
The `ColorTheme` and `Variant` enums are removed from ThemeContext.tsx
The `.theme-purple`, `.theme-lime`, `.theme-red`, `.theme-lavender`, `.theme-orange`, `.theme-green` CSS blocks (and their `.dark` counterparts) are removed from global.css
The `ignoreMembers` entries for theme enum values in knip.config.ts are removed (no longer needed)
The `getInitialColorTheme`, `getInitialVariant` functions and their localStorage reads are removed
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
The ThemeContext (docs/contexts/ThemeContext.tsx) manages color theme and variant state that is never consumed:

- `colorTheme` state + `setColorTheme` function + `ColorTheme` enum (Purple, Lime, Red, Lavender, Orange, Green) — no component reads `colorTheme` or calls `setColorTheme`
- `variant` state + `setVariant` function + `Variant` enum — never consumed
- `getInitialColorTheme()` and `getInitialVariant()` localStorage reads — unnecessary

The CSS (docs/app/global.css) has ~200 lines of `.theme-*` class definitions (lines 94-326) that are never applied to any DOM element.

The knip.config.ts (docs/knip.config.ts lines 27-36) suppresses warnings for the unused enum members.

Remove all of this. Keep only `isDarkMode`, `toggleDarkMode`, and the `.dark` class toggle logic. The `:root` and `.dark` theme blocks stay (they are actively used). The 6 color theme variant blocks go.

Be careful: the `ThemeProvider` is used in layout.tsx and `useTheme` is used in TopNav.tsx — both only need `isDarkMode` and `toggleDarkMode`.
